summary: The team is fully focused on the joint CKGSB-IE 'China Going Global' report with a September launch, while simultaneously executing the Asia Start campaign with dual AI and traditional routes and preparing the new Intelligent China program for December.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint report with IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. The team is racing to finalize the structure, secure op-eds from President Santiago and Dean, translate the Chinese report and alumni case studies, and meet with Tech in Asia tomorrow to confirm the launch event. This week, Felix must deliver the one-pager proposal, Tyson must lock down the event theme, and the team must complete translations. Meanwhile, the Asia Start campaign is running two parallel tracks—AI-driven (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy)—for the first month, with evaluation to follow. The new 'Intelligent China' program, launching in December, requires a webpage and SCM campaign plan from Joseph and the team. Additionally, Ira needs to revise the 'Disruptive Timing' article and finalize the Dean's article titled 'China's Gateway', while Joseph reviews the report plan and translates Prof Li Wei's FT piece. The UNICON proposal review is also due.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discusses a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. They plan to include a main research piece from Professor Leeway, op-eds from leadership, and industry case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign results and discussed the new 'Intelligent China' program launching in December, focusing on module one in Beijing. Decided to run two routes for Asia Start: AI-driven (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy), then evaluate.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation and Market Dynamics
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping market cooperation.

decisions.text: The report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools' leadership.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The working title is 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The report will be launched in September, with a digital version ready for events in Singapore.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Industry perspective will be included via case studies from the Chinese report or translated alumni articles.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-driven and traditional, then decide on one for the remainder.
The new program is named 'Intelligent China' and focuses on module one in Beijing, targeting foreigners.
Chosen title for article on behalf of Dean: 'China's Gateway'.

decisions.text: Professor Li Wei agreed to collaborate with IE on the joint report on Chinese companies going global.
